(Research and document pytest output formats)  \n4\t**Date:** 2026-07-14  \n5\t**Status:** ✅ Complete\n6\t\n7\t## Overview\n8\t\n9\tThis document analyzes the three primary pytest output formats collected from actual test runs and documents the exact patterns for extracting file paths, line numbers, expected values, and actual values.\n10\t\n11\t## The Three Sample Formats\n12\t\n13\tBased on the parent bead (bf-1ym8jw) sample collection, pytest produces three distinct output formats depending on flags:\n14\t\n15\t### Format 1: Full Detailed Format (`-vv --tb=short`)\n16\t\n17\t**Flags:** `pytest -vv --tb=short`  \n18\t**Characteristics:** Complete diffs, multi-line assertions, detailed context\n19\t\n20\t#### Sample Output\n21\t\n22\t```\n23\t_____________________________ test_simple_equality _____________________________\n24\ttools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: in test_simple_equality\n25\t    assert greeting == \"world\", f\"Expected 'world', got '{greeting}'\"\n26\tE   AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'\n27\tE   assert 'hello' == 'world'\n28\tE     \n29\tE     - world\n30\tE     + hello\n31\t______________________________ test_dict_equality ______________________________\n32\ttools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:24: in test_dict_equality\n33\t    assert actual == expected, f\"Dictionaries don't match\"\n34\tE   AssertionError: Dictionaries don't match\n35\tE   assert {'name': 'Bob', 'age': 25, 'city': 'LA'} == {'name': 'Alice', 'age': 30, 'city': 'NYC'}\n36\tE     \n37\tE     Differing items:\n38\tE     {'city': 'LA'} != {'city': 'NYC'}\n39\tE     {'age': 25} != {'age': 30}\n40\tE     {'name': 'Bob'} != {'name': 'Alice'}\n41\tE     \n42\tE     Full diff:\n43\tE       {\n44\tE     -     'age': 30,\n45\tE     ?            ^^\n46\tE     +     'age': 25,\n47\tE     ?            ^^\n48\tE     -     'city': 'NYC',\n49\tE     ?              ^^^\n50\tE     +     'city': 'LA',\n51\tE     ?              ^^\n52\tE     -     'name': 'Alice',\n53\tE     ?              ^^^^^\n54\tE     +     'name': 'Bob',\n55\tE     ?              ^^^\n56\tE       }\n57\t_____________________________ test_list_comparison _____________________________\n58\ttools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:31: in test_list_comparison\n59\t    assert list1 == list2, \"Lists should be equal\"\n60\tE   AssertionError: Lists should be equal\n61\tE   assert [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] == [1, 2, 10, 4, 5]\n62\tE     \n63\tE     At index 2 diff: 3 != 10\n64\tE     \n65\tE     Full diff:\n66\tE       [\n67\tE           1,\n68\tE           2,\n69\tE     -     10,\n70\tE     ?     ^^\n71\tE     +     3,\n72\tE     ?     ^\n73\tE           4,\n74\tE           5,\n75\tE       ]\n76\t```\n77\t\n78\t### Format 2: Long Format with Code Context (`-v --tb=long`)\n79\t\n80\t**Flags:** `pytest -v --tb=long`  \n81\t**Characteristics:** Shows source code context, truncates diffs with `-v` (needs `-vv` for complete diffs)\n82\t\n83\t#### Sample Output\n84\t\n85\t```\n86\t_____________________________ test_simple_equality _____________________________\n87\t\n88\t    def test_simple_equality():\n89\t        \"\"\"Simple equality assertion failure.\"\"\"\n90\t        greeting = \"hello\"\n91\t>       assert greeting == \"world\", f\"Expected 'world', got '{greeting}'\"\n92\tE       AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'\n93\tE       assert 'hello' == 'world'\n94\tE         \n95\tE         - world\n96\tE         + hello\n97\t\n98\ttools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: AssertionError\n99\t______________________________ test_dict_equality ______________________________\n100\t\n101\t    def test_dict_equality():\n102\t        \"\"\"Dictionary equality assertion failure.\"\"\"\n103\t        expected = {\"name\": \"Alice\", \"age\": 30, \"city\": \"NYC\"}\n104\t        actual = {\"name\": \"Bob\", \"age\": 25, \"city\": \"LA\"}\n105\t>       assert actual == expected, f\"Dictionaries don't match\"\n106\tE       AssertionError: Dictionaries don't match\n107\tE       assert {'age': 25, '...'name': 'Bob'} == {'age': 30, '...ame': 'Alice'}\n108\tE         \n109\tE         Differing items:\n110\tE         {'city': 'LA'} != {'city': 'NYC'}\n111\tE         {'age': 25} != {'age': 30}\n112\tE         {'name': 'Bob'} != {'name': 'Alice'}\n113\tE         \n114\tE         Full diff:...\n115\tE         \n116\tE         ...Full output truncated (14 lines hidden), use '-vv' to show\n117\t\n118\ttools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:24: AssertionError\n119\t_____________________________ test_list_comparison _____________________________\n120\t\n121\t    def test_list_comparison():\n122\t        \"\"\"List comparison with index diff.\"\"\"\n123\t        list1 = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]\n124\t        list2 = [1, 2, 10, 4, 5]\n125\t>       assert list1 == list2, \"Lists should be equal\"\n126\tE       AssertionError: Lists should be equal\n127\tE       assert [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] == [1, 2, 10, 4, 5]\n128\tE         \n129\tE         At index 2 diff: 3 != 10\n130\tE         \n131\tE         Full diff:\n132\tE           [\n133\tE               1,\n134\tE               2,...\n135\tE         \n136\tE         ...Full output truncated (7 lines hidden), use '-vv' to show\n137\t\n138\ttools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:31: AssertionError\n139\t```\n140\t\n141\t### Format 3: Single Line Format (`--tb=line`)\n142\t\n143\t**Flags:** `pytest --tb=line`  \n144\t**Characteristics:** One line per failure, most parseable, minimal context\n145\t\n146\t#### Sample Output\n147\t\n148\t```\n149\t=================================== FAILURES ===================================\n150\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'\n151\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:24: AssertionError: Dictionaries don't match\n152\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:31: AssertionError: Lists should be equal\n153\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:44: AssertionError: assert '\\n    This i...ontent.\\n    ' == '\\n    This i... lines.\\n    '\n154\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:51: AssertionError: Numbers don't match: 5 != 10\n155\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:57: AssertionError: orange should be in the list\n156\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:64: AssertionError: Sequences should match\n157\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:81: AssertionError: Nested structures should match\n158\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:89: AssertionError: Floats don't match: 0.30000000000000004 != 0.3\n159\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:96: AssertionError: Both x and y should be True\n160\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:102: AssertionError: Uppercase conversion failed\n161\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:108: AssertionError: Value should be int, got str\n162\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:115: AssertionError: Sets should be equal\n163\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:122: AssertionError: Ranges should be equal\n164\t/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:129: AssertionError: Tuples should be equal\n165\t```\n166\t\n167\t## Common Patterns Across All Formats\n168\t\n169\t### 1. File Location Pattern\n170\t\n171\t**Regex:** `r'^(.+?):(\\d+):'`\n172\t\n173\t**Extracts:**\n174\t- Group 1: File path (e.g., `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py` or `/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py`)\n175\t- Group 2: Line number (e.g., `17`, `24`, `31`)\n176\t\n177\t**Works in all three formats:**\n178\t- Format 1: `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17:`\n179\t- Format 2: `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17:`\n180\t- Format 3: `/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17:`\n181\t\n182\t### 2. Test Name Pattern (Formats 1 & 2)\n183\t\n184\t**Regex:** `r'in\\s+(\\w+)'`\n185\t\n186\t**Extracts:**\n187\t- Group 1: Test function name (e.g., `test_simple_equality`, `test_dict_equality`)\n188\t\n189\t**Works in:**\n190\t- Format 1: `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: in test_simple_equality`\n191\t- Format 2: `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: in test_simple_equality`\n192\t- Format 3: Not available\n193\t\n194\t### 3. Error Message Pattern (Format 3)\n195\t\n196\t**Regex:** `r'AssertionError:\\s*(.+)'`\n197\t\n198\t**Extracts:**\n199\t- Group 1: Full error message (e.g., `Expected 'world', got 'hello'`)\n200\t\n201\t**Works in:**\n202\t- Format 3: `/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'`\n203\t- Formats 1 & 2: `E   AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'` (needs `E   ` prefix handling)\n204\t\n205\t## Assertion-Specific Patterns\n206\t\n207\t### Equality Assertion Pattern\n208\t\n209\t**Regex:** `r'assert\\s+(.+?)\\s+==\\s+(.+)'`\n210\t\n211\t**Extracts:**\n212\t- Group 1: Actual value (left side)\n213\t- Group 2: Expected value (right side)\n214\t\n215\t**Examples:**\n216\t- `assert 'hello' == 'world'` → actual=`'hello'`, expected=`'world'`\n217\t- `assert 5 == 10` → actual=`5`, expected=`10`\n218\t- `assert [1, 2, 3] == [1, 2, 10]` → actual=`[1, 2, 3]`, expected=`[1, 2, 10]`\n219\t\n220\t### Index Diff Pattern\n221\t\n222\t**Regex:** `r'At\\s+index\\s+(\\d+)\\s+diff:\\s+(.+?)\\s+!=\\s+(.+)'`\n223\t\n224\t**Extracts:**\n225\t- Group 1: Index position (e.g., `2`, `5`)\n226\t- Group 2: Actual value at index\n227\t- Group 3: Expected value at index\n228\t\n229\t**Examples:**\n230\t- `At index 2 diff: 3 != 10` → index=`2`, actual=`3`, expected=`10`\n231\t- `At index 5 diff: 10 != 5` → index=`5`, actual=`10`, expected=`5`\n232\t\n233\t### Differing Items Pattern\n234\t\n235\t**Regex:** `r'\\{(.+?)\\}\\s+!=\\s+\\{(.+?)\\}'`\n236\t\n237\t**Extracts:**\n238\t- Group 1: Left side (actual) key-value\n239\t- Group 2: Right side (expected) key-value\n240\t\n241\t**Examples:**\n242\t- `{'city': 'LA'} != {'city': 'NYC'}` → actual=`{'city': 'LA'}`, expected=`{'city': 'NYC'}`\n243\t- `{'age': 25} != {'age': 30}` → actual=`{'age': 25}`, expected=`{'age': 30}`\n244\t\n245\t### Set Operations Pattern\n246\t\n247\t**Left set regex:** `r'Extra items in the left set:'`  \n248\t**Right set regex:** `r'Extra items in the right set:'`\n249\t\n250\t**Followed by values on subsequent lines:**\n251\t- `4` (in left set)\n252\t- `5` (in right set)\n253\t\n254\t### Range Diff Pattern\n255\t\n256\t**Regex:** `r'(Right|Left) contains (?:one more item|\\d+ more items?)\\s*:\\s*(.+)'`\n257\t\n258\t**Extracts:**\n259\t- Group 1: Side (Right/Left)\n260\t- Group 2: Extra item value\n261\t\n262\t**Examples:**\n263\t- `Right contains one more item: 10` → side=`Right`, item=`10`\n264\t- `Left contains 2 more items: 3, 4` → side=`Left`, items=`3, 4`\n265\t\n266\t### Where Clause Pattern (Type Checks)\n267\t\n268\t**Regex:** `r'where\\s+(.+?)\\s+=\\s+(.+)'`\n269\t\n270\t**Extracts:**\n271\t- Group 1: Expression (e.g., `False`)\n272\t- Group 2: Computation (e.g., `isinstance('123', int)`)\n273\t\n274\t**Examples:**\n275\t- `where False = isinstance('123', int)` → expr=`False`, computation=`isinstance('123', int)`\n276\t\n277\t## Expected/Actual Extraction from Diffs\n278\t\n279\t### Diff Line Markers\n280\t\n281\t**Expected (minus) pattern:** `r'^\\s*-\\s*(.+)'`  \n282\t**Actual (plus) pattern:** `r'^\\s*\\+\\s*(.+)'`\n283\t\n284\t**Extracts:**\n285\t- Group 1: The value on that line\n286\t\n287\t**Examples:**\n288\t- `    - world` → `world`\n289\t- `    + hello` → `hello`\n290\t- `  -     'age': 30,` → `'age': 30,`\n291\t- `  +     'age': 25,` → `'age': 25,`\n292\t\n293\t### Position Markers\n294\t\n295\t**Regex:** `r'^\\s*\\?\\s+(.+)'`\n296\t\n297\t**Extracts:**\n298\t- Group 1: Position indicator (shows which characters differ)\n299\t\n300\t**Examples:**\n301\t- `?            ^^` → positions 2-3 differ\n302\t- `?              ^^^` → positions 3-5 differ\n303\t\n304\t## Edge Cases and Format Variations\n305\t\n306\t### 1. Truncated Output\n307\t\n308\t**Pattern:** `r'\\.\\.\\.Full output truncated\\s+\\((\\d+) lines hidden\\)'`  \n309\t**Occurs in:** Format 2 when using `-v` without `-vv`  \n310\t**Example:** `...Full output truncated (14 lines hidden), use '-vv' to show`\n311\t\n312\t### 2. Ellipsis in Values\n313\t\n314\t**Pattern:** Three consecutive dots in truncated values  \n315\t**Occurs in:** Format 2 and Format 3 for long values  \n316\t**Examples:**\n317\t- `assert '\\n    This i...ontent.\\n    ' == '\\n    This i... lines.\\n    '`\n318\t- `assert [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 10, ...] == [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, ...]`\n319\t\n320\t### 3. Multiline String Escapes\n321\t\n322\t**Pattern:** Escape sequences like `\\n`, `\\t` in string diffs  \n323\t**Occurs in:** All formats  \n324\t**Example:** `assert '\\n    This i...ontent.\\n    ' == '\\n    This i... lines.\\n    '`\n325\t\n326\t### 4. Path Format Variations\n327\t\n328\t**Relative paths** (Formats 1 & 2): `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17`  \n329\t**Absolute paths** (Format 3): `/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17`\n330\t\n331\t**Solution:** Use `os.path.normpath()` for consistent path handling.\n332\t\n333\t### 5. Whitespace Variations\n334\t\n335\t**Format 1 & 2:** `E   ` prefix (3 spaces after E)  \n336\t**Format 3:** No prefix, direct text\n337\t\n338\t**Pattern variations:**\n339\t- `E   AssertionError:` (Format 1 & 2)\n340\t- `AssertionError:` (Format 3)\n341\t- `E   assert` (Format 1 & 2)\n342\t- `assert` (implied in Format 3)\n343\t\n344\t### 6. Floating-Point Precision\n345\t\n346\t**Pattern:** Numbers like `0.30000000000000004` vs `0.3`  \n347\t**Occurs in:** All formats  \n348\t**Example:** `AssertionError: Floats don't match: 0.30000000000000004 != 0.3`\n349\t\n350\t### 7. Boolean Logic\n351\t\n352\t**Pattern:** Complex expressions without explicit expected/actual  \n353\t**Example:** `assert (True and False)`  \n354\t**Note:** No diff shown, just the assertion\n355\t\n356\t### 8. Nested Structure Truncation\n357\t\n358\t**Pattern:** Deep nesting gets truncated in Format 2  \n359\t**Example:** `assert {'users': [{'...92, 87, 91]}]} == {'users': [{'...92, 99, 91]}]}`  \n360\t**Note:** Use `-vv` for complete nested diffs\n361\t\n362\t## Recommended Parsing Strategy by Format\n363\t\n364\t### Format 1: Full Detailed (`-vv --tb=short`)\n365\t\n366\t**Parse order:**\n367\t1. Extract file:line using `r'^(.+?):(\\d+):'`\n368\t2. Extract test name using `r'in\\s+(\\w+)'`\n369\t3. Extract assertion using `r'assert\\s+(.+)'`\n370\t4. Classify assertion type (equality, contains, isinstance, etc.)\n371\t5. Extract diff lines using `r'^\\s*[-+]\\s*(.+)'`\n372\t6. Parse expected from `-` lines, actual from `+` lines\n373\t\n374\t### Format 2: Long Context (`-v --tb=long`)\n375\t\n376\t**Parse order:**\n377\t1. Extract file:line using `r'^(.+?):(\\d+):'`\n378\t2. Extract test name using `r'in\\s+(\\w+)'`\n379\t3. Look for `>` marker to find failing line\n380\t4. Parse assertion from line with `>`\n381\t5. Handle truncated output warnings\n382\t6. Extract available diffs (may be incomplete)\n383\t\n384\t### Format 3: Single Line (`--tb=line`)\n385\t\n386\t**Parse order:**\n387\t1. Extract file:line using `r'^(.+?):(\\d+):'`\n388\t2. Extract error message using `r'AssertionError:\\s*(.+)'`\n389\t3. Parse expected/actual from message if available (e.g., \"Expected X, got Y\")\n390\t4. No test name available in this format\n391\t\n392\t## Summary Table\n393\t\n394\t| Field | Format 1 | Format 2 | Format 3 | Pattern |\n395\t|-------|----------|----------|----------|---------|\n396\t| File path | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | `r'^(.+?):(\\d+):'` |\n397\t| Line number | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | Same as above |\n398\t| Test name | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | ❌ No | `r'in\\s+(\\w+)'` |\n399\t| Error message | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | `r'AssertionError:\\s*(.+)'` |\n400\t| Expected value | ✅ Yes | ⚠️ Partial | ❌ No | From `-` diff lines |\n401\t| Actual value | ✅ Yes | ⚠️ Partial | ❌ No | From `+` diff lines |\n402\t| Index diffs | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | ❌ No | `r'At\\s+index\\s+(\\d+)'` |\n403\t| Dict diffs | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | ❌ No | `r'Differing items:'` |\n404\t| Complete diffs | ✅ Yes | ⚠️ Truncated | ❌ No | N/A |\n405\t\n406\t## Conclusion\n407\t\n408\tThe three formats serve different purposes:\n409\t\n410\t- **Format 1 (`-vv --tb=short`)**: Best for detailed value extraction and debugging\n411\t- **Format 2 (`-v --tb=long`)**: Good for understanding code context, but diffs are truncated\n412\t- **Format 3 (`--tb=line`)**: Best for quick status checks and failure counting\n413\t\n414\tFor ARMOR's test failure analysis system, **Format 1** provides the most complete information for extracting file paths, line numbers, expected values, and actual Pytest Output Format Patterns\n\n**Task:** bf-5x5xz1 (Research and document pytest output formats)  \n**Date:** 2026-07-14  \n**Status:** ✅ Complete\n\n## Overview\n\nThis document analyzes the three primary pytest output formats collected from actual test runs and documents the exact patterns for extracting file paths, line numbers, expected values, and actual values.\n\n## The Three Sample Formats\n\nBased on the parent bead (bf-1ym8jw) sample collection, pytest produces three distinct output formats depending on flags:\n\n### Format 1: Full Detailed Format (`-vv --tb=short`)\n\n**Flags:** `pytest -vv --tb=short`  \n**Characteristics:** Complete diffs, multi-line assertions, detailed context\n\n#### Sample Output\n\n```\n_____________________________ test_simple_equality _____________________________\ntools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: in test_simple_equality\n    assert greeting == \"world\", f\"Expected 'world', got '{greeting}'\"\nE   AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'\nE   assert 'hello' == 'world'\nE     \nE     - world\nE     + hello\n______________________________ test_dict_equality ______________________________\ntools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:24: in test_dict_equality\n    assert actual == expected, f\"Dictionaries don't match\"\nE   AssertionError: Dictionaries don't match\nE   assert {'name': 'Bob', 'age': 25, 'city': 'LA'} == {'name': 'Alice', 'age': 30, 'city': 'NYC'}\nE     \nE     Differing items:\nE     {'city': 'LA'} != {'city': 'NYC'}\nE     {'age': 25} != {'age': 30}\nE     {'name': 'Bob'} != {'name': 'Alice'}\nE     \nE     Full diff:\nE       {\nE     -     'age': 30,\nE     ?            ^^\nE     +     'age': 25,\nE     ?            ^^\nE     -     'city': 'NYC',\nE     ?              ^^^\nE     +     'city': 'LA',\nE     ?              ^^\nE     -     'name': 'Alice',\nE     ?              ^^^^^\nE     +     'name': 'Bob',\nE     ?              ^^^\nE       }\n_____________________________ test_list_comparison _____________________________\ntools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:31: in test_list_comparison\n    assert list1 == list2, \"Lists should be equal\"\nE   AssertionError: Lists should be equal\nE   assert [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] == [1, 2, 10, 4, 5]\nE     \nE     At index 2 diff: 3 != 10\nE     \nE     Full diff:\nE       [\nE           1,\nE           2,\nE     -     10,\nE     ?     ^^\nE     +     3,\nE     ?     ^\nE           4,\nE           5,\nE       ]\n```\n\n### Format 2: Long Format with Code Context (`-v --tb=long`)\n\n**Flags:** `pytest -v --tb=long`  \n**Characteristics:** Shows source code context, truncates diffs with `-v` (needs `-vv` for complete diffs)\n\n#### Sample Output\n\n```\n_____________________________ test_simple_equality _____________________________\n\n    def test_simple_equality():\n        \"\"\"Simple equality assertion failure.\"\"\"\n        greeting = \"hello\"\n>       assert greeting == \"world\", f\"Expected 'world', got '{greeting}'\"\nE       AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'\nE       assert 'hello' == 'world'\nE         \nE         - world\nE         + hello\n\ntools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: AssertionError\n______________________________ test_dict_equality ______________________________\n\n    def test_dict_equality():\n        \"\"\"Dictionary equality assertion failure.\"\"\"\n        expected = {\"name\": \"Alice\", \"age\": 30, \"city\": \"NYC\"}\n        actual = {\"name\": \"Bob\", \"age\": 25, \"city\": \"LA\"}\n>       assert actual == expected, f\"Dictionaries don't match\"\nE       AssertionError: Dictionaries don't match\nE       assert {'age': 25, '...'name': 'Bob'} == {'age': 30, '...ame': 'Alice'}\nE         \nE         Differing items:\nE         {'city': 'LA'} != {'city': 'NYC'}\nE         {'age': 25} != {'age': 30}\nE         {'name': 'Bob'} != {'name': 'Alice'}\nE         \nE         Full diff:...\nE         \nE         ...Full output truncated (14 lines hidden), use '-vv' to show\n\ntools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:24: AssertionError\n_____________________________ test_list_comparison _____________________________\n\n    def test_list_comparison():\n        \"\"\"List comparison with index diff.\"\"\"\n        list1 = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]\n        list2 = [1, 2, 10, 4, 5]\n>       assert list1 == list2, \"Lists should be equal\"\nE       AssertionError: Lists should be equal\nE       assert [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] == [1, 2, 10, 4, 5]\nE         \nE         At index 2 diff: 3 != 10\nE         \nE         Full diff:\nE           [\nE               1,\nE               2,...\nE         \nE         ...Full output truncated (7 lines hidden), use '-vv' to show\n\ntools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:31: AssertionError\n```\n\n### Format 3: Single Line Format (`--tb=line`)\n\n**Flags:** `pytest --tb=line`  \n**Characteristics:** One line per failure, most parseable, minimal context\n\n#### Sample Output\n\n```\n=================================== FAILURES ===================================\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:24: AssertionError: Dictionaries don't match\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:31: AssertionError: Lists should be equal\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:44: AssertionError: assert '\\n    This i...ontent.\\n    ' == '\\n    This i... lines.\\n    '\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:51: AssertionError: Numbers don't match: 5 != 10\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:57: AssertionError: orange should be in the list\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:64: AssertionError: Sequences should match\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:81: AssertionError: Nested structures should match\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:89: AssertionError: Floats don't match: 0.30000000000000004 != 0.3\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:96: AssertionError: Both x and y should be True\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:102: AssertionError: Uppercase conversion failed\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:108: AssertionError: Value should be int, got str\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:115: AssertionError: Sets should be equal\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:122: AssertionError: Ranges should be equal\n/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:129: AssertionError: Tuples should be equal\n```\n\n## Common Patterns Across All Formats\n\n### 1. File Location Pattern\n\n**Regex:** `r'^(.+?):(\\d+):'`\n\n**Extracts:**\n- Group 1: File path (e.g., `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py` or `/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py`)\n- Group 2: Line number (e.g., `17`, `24`, `31`)\n\n**Works in all three formats:**\n- Format 1: `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17:`\n- Format 2: `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17:`\n- Format 3: `/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17:`\n\n### 2. Test Name Pattern (Formats 1 & 2)\n\n**Regex:** `r'in\\s+(\\w+)'`\n\n**Extracts:**\n- Group 1: Test function name (e.g., `test_simple_equality`, `test_dict_equality`)\n\n**Works in:**\n- Format 1: `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: in test_simple_equality`\n- Format 2: `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: in test_simple_equality`\n- Format 3: Not available\n\n### 3. Error Message Pattern (Format 3)\n\n**Regex:** `r'AssertionError:\\s*(.+)'`\n\n**Extracts:**\n- Group 1: Full error message (e.g., `Expected 'world', got 'hello'`)\n\n**Works in:**\n- Format 3: `/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17: AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'`\n- Formats 1 & 2: `E   AssertionError: Expected 'world', got 'hello'` (needs `E   ` prefix handling)\n\n## Assertion-Specific Patterns\n\n### Equality Assertion Pattern\n\n**Regex:** `r'assert\\s+(.+?)\\s+==\\s+(.+)'`\n\n**Extracts:**\n- Group 1: Actual value (left side)\n- Group 2: Expected value (right side)\n\n**Examples:**\n- `assert 'hello' == 'world'` → actual=`'hello'`, expected=`'world'`\n- `assert 5 == 10` → actual=`5`, expected=`10`\n- `assert [1, 2, 3] == [1, 2, 10]` → actual=`[1, 2, 3]`, expected=`[1, 2, 10]`\n\n### Index Diff Pattern\n\n**Regex:** `r'At\\s+index\\s+(\\d+)\\s+diff:\\s+(.+?)\\s+!=\\s+(.+)'`\n\n**Extracts:**\n- Group 1: Index position (e.g., `2`, `5`)\n- Group 2: Actual value at index\n- Group 3: Expected value at index\n\n**Examples:**\n- `At index 2 diff: 3 != 10` → index=`2`, actual=`3`, expected=`10`\n- `At index 5 diff: 10 != 5` → index=`5`, actual=`10`, expected=`5`\n\n### Differing Items Pattern\n\n**Regex:** `r'\\{(.+?)\\}\\s+!=\\s+\\{(.+?)\\}'`\n\n**Extracts:**\n- Group 1: Left side (actual) key-value\n- Group 2: Right side (expected) key-value\n\n**Examples:**\n- `{'city': 'LA'} != {'city': 'NYC'}` → actual=`{'city': 'LA'}`, expected=`{'city': 'NYC'}`\n- `{'age': 25} != {'age': 30}` → actual=`{'age': 25}`, expected=`{'age': 30}`\n\n### Set Operations Pattern\n\n**Left set regex:** `r'Extra items in the left set:'`  \n**Right set regex:** `r'Extra items in the right set:'`\n\n**Followed by values on subsequent lines:**\n- `4` (in left set)\n- `5` (in right set)\n\n### Range Diff Pattern\n\n**Regex:** `r'(Right|Left) contains (?:one more item|\\d+ more items?)\\s*:\\s*(.+)'`\n\n**Extracts:**\n- Group 1: Side (Right/Left)\n- Group 2: Extra item value\n\n**Examples:**\n- `Right contains one more item: 10` → side=`Right`, item=`10`\n- `Left contains 2 more items: 3, 4` → side=`Left`, items=`3, 4`\n\n### Where Clause Pattern (Type Checks)\n\n**Regex:** `r'where\\s+(.+?)\\s+=\\s+(.+)'`\n\n**Extracts:**\n- Group 1: Expression (e.g., `False`)\n- Group 2: Computation (e.g., `isinstance('123', int)`)\n\n**Examples:**\n- `where False = isinstance('123', int)` → expr=`False`, computation=`isinstance('123', int)`\n\n## Expected/Actual Extraction from Diffs\n\n### Diff Line Markers\n\n**Expected (minus) pattern:** `r'^\\s*-\\s*(.+)'`  \n**Actual (plus) pattern:** `r'^\\s*\\+\\s*(.+)'`\n\n**Extracts:**\n- Group 1: The value on that line\n\n**Examples:**\n- `    - world` → `world`\n- `    + hello` → `hello`\n- `  -     'age': 30,` → `'age': 30,`\n- `  +     'age': 25,` → `'age': 25,`\n\n### Position Markers\n\n**Regex:** `r'^\\s*\\?\\s+(.+)'`\n\n**Extracts:**\n- Group 1: Position indicator (shows which characters differ)\n\n**Examples:**\n- `?            ^^` → positions 2-3 differ\n- `?              ^^^` → positions 3-5 differ\n\n## Edge Cases and Format Variations\n\n### 1. Truncated Output\n\n**Pattern:** `r'\\.\\.\\.Full output truncated\\s+\\((\\d+) lines hidden\\)'`  \n**Occurs in:** Format 2 when using `-v` without `-vv`  \n**Example:** `...Full output truncated (14 lines hidden), use '-vv' to show`\n\n### 2. Ellipsis in Values\n\n**Pattern:** Three consecutive dots in truncated values  \n**Occurs in:** Format 2 and Format 3 for long values  \n**Examples:**\n- `assert '\\n    This i...ontent.\\n    ' == '\\n    This i... lines.\\n    '`\n- `assert [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 10, ...] == [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, ...]`\n\n### 3. Multiline String Escapes\n\n**Pattern:** Escape sequences like `\\n`, `\\t` in string diffs  \n**Occurs in:** All formats  \n**Example:** `assert '\\n    This i...ontent.\\n    ' == '\\n    This i... lines.\\n    '`\n\n### 4. Path Format Variations\n\n**Relative paths** (Formats 1 & 2): `tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17`  \n**Absolute paths** (Format 3): `/home/coding/ARMOR/tools/test_pytest_flags_minimal.py:17`\n\n**Solution:** Use `os.path.normpath()` for consistent path handling.\n\n### 5. Whitespace Variations\n\n**Format 1 & 2:** `E   ` prefix (3 spaces after E)  \n**Format 3:** No prefix, direct text\n\n**Pattern variations:**\n- `E   AssertionError:` (Format 1 & 2)\n- `AssertionError:` (Format 3)\n- `E   assert` (Format 1 & 2)\n- `assert` (implied in Format 3)\n\n### 6. Floating-Point Precision\n\n**Pattern:** Numbers like `0.30000000000000004` vs `0.3`  \n**Occurs in:** All formats  \n**Example:** `AssertionError: Floats don't match: 0.30000000000000004 != 0.3`\n\n### 7. Boolean Logic\n\n**Pattern:** Complex expressions without explicit expected/actual  \n**Example:** `assert (True and False)`  \n**Note:** No diff shown, just the assertion\n\n### 8. Nested Structure Truncation\n\n**Pattern:** Deep nesting gets truncated in Format 2  \n**Example:** `assert {'users': [{'...92, 87, 91]}]} == {'users': [{'...92, 99, 91]}]}`  \n**Note:** Use `-vv` for complete nested diffs\n\n## Recommended Parsing Strategy by Format\n\n### Format 1: Full Detailed (`-vv --tb=short`)\n\n**Parse order:**\n1. Extract file:line using `r'^(.+?):(\\d+):'`\n2. Extract test name using `r'in\\s+(\\w+)'`\n3. Extract assertion using `r'assert\\s+(.+)'`\n4. Classify assertion type (equality, contains, isinstance, etc.)\n5. Extract diff lines using `r'^\\s*[-+]\\s*(.+)'`\n6. Parse expected from `-` lines, actual from `+` lines\n\n### Format 2: Long Context (`-v --tb=long`)\n\n**Parse order:**\n1. Extract file:line using `r'^(.+?):(\\d+):'`\n2. Extract test name using `r'in\\s+(\\w+)'`\n3. Look for `>` marker to find failing line\n4. Parse assertion from line with `>`\n5. Handle truncated output warnings\n6. Extract available diffs (may be incomplete)\n\n### Format 3: Single Line (`--tb=line`)\n\n**Parse order:**\n1. Extract file:line using `r'^(.+?):(\\d+):'`\n2. Extract error message using `r'AssertionError:\\s*(.+)'`\n3. Parse expected/actual from message if available (e.g., \"Expected X, got Y\")\n4. No test name available in this format\n\n## Summary Table\n\n| Field | Format 1 | Format 2 | Format 3 | Pattern |\n|-------|----------|----------|----------|---------|\n| File path | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | `r'^(.+?):(\\d+):'` |\n| Line number | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | Same as above |\n| Test name | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | ❌ No | `r'in\\s+(\\w+)'` |\n| Error message | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | `r'AssertionError:\\s*(.+)'` |\n| Expected value | ✅ Yes | ⚠️ Partial | ❌ No | From `-` diff lines |\n| Actual value | ✅ Yes | ⚠️ Partial | ❌ No | From `+` diff lines |\n| Index diffs | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | ❌ No | `r'At\\s+index\\s+(\\d+)'` |\n| Dict diffs | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | ❌ No | `r'Differing items:'` |\n| Complete diffs | ✅ Yes | ⚠️ Truncated | ❌ No | N/A |\n\n## Conclusion\n\nThe three formats serve different purposes:\n\n- **Format 1 (`-vv --tb=short`)**: Best for detailed value extraction and debugging\n- **Format 2 (`-v --tb=long`)**: Good for understanding code context, but diffs are truncated\n- **Format 3 (`--tb=line`)**: Best for quick status checks and failure counting\n\nFor ARMOR's test failure analysis system, **Format 1** provides the most complete information for extracting file paths, line numbers, expected values, and actual values.\n\n---\n\n**Generated:** 2026-07-14 for ARMOR project (bead: bf-5x5xz1)  \n**Parent Task:** bf-1ym8jw (Collect sample pytest failure outputs)  \n**Source Samples:** Collected from actual pytest runs on test_pytest_flags_minimal.py\n","numLines":421,"startLine":1,"totalLines":421}}}

ASSERT_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s+assert\\s+(.+)'\n70\t    EQUALITY_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s+assert\\s+(.+?)\\s+==\\s+(.+)'\n71\t    CONTAINS_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s+assert\\s+(.+?)\\s+in\\s+(.+)'\n72\t    TYPE_CHECK_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s+assert\\s+isinstance\\((.+?),\\s*(.+?)\\)'\n73\t\n74\t    # Diff patterns\n75\t    DIFF_MINUS_PATTERN = r'^\\s*-\\s*(.+)'\n76\t    DIFF_PLUS_PATTERN = Handles multiple pytest output formats:\n100\t        - --tb=line: One line per failure with file:line: AssertionError: message\n101\t        - --tb=short: Multi-line failures with detailed diffs\n102\t        - --tb=no: Only summary section\n103\t\n104\t        Args:\n105\t            output: Raw pytest output string\n106\t\n107\t        Returns:\n108\t            List of TestFailure self._parse_line_format(output):\n114\t            return self.failures\n115\t\n116\t        # Otherwise, parse with --tb=short format (has underscore headers)\n117\t        self._parse_short_format(output)\n118\t\n119\t        return self.failures\n120\t\n121\t    def _parse_line_format(self, output: str) -> bool:\n122\t        \"\"\"\n123\t        Parse --tb=line format.\n124\t\n125\t        Format: file:line: AssertionError: message\n126\t        Each failure is on a single line.\n127\t        \"\"\"\n128\t        lines = output.split('\\n')\n129\t               )\n147\t\n148\t                # Try to extract expected/actual from error message\n149\t                self._extract_error_message_details(failure)\n150\t\n151\t                self.failures.append(failure)\n152\t\n153\t        return failures_found\n154\t\n155\t    def _parse_short_format(self, output: str) -> None:\n156\t        \"\"\"\n157\t        Parse --tb=short format with underscore headers.\n158\t\n159\t        Format:\n160\t        _________________________ test_name ___________________________\n161\t        file:line: in test_name\n162\t            assert current_failure.actual = match.group(3).strip()\n222\t                continue\n223\t\n224\t            # Detect differing items section\n225\t            if re.match(self.DICT_DIFF_HEADER,           try:\n236\t                        left_dict = self._parse_dict_value(match.group(1))\n237\t                        right_dict = self._parse_dict_value(match.group(2))\n238\t r'^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):\\s+in\\s+(\\w+)'\n    LINE_FAILURE_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):\\s+AssertionError:\\s*(.+)'\n    VERBOSE_TEST_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?)::(\\w+)\\s+(FAILED|PASSED|ERROR|SKIPPED)\\s+\\[\\s*\\d+%?\\]'\n    CONCISE_TEST_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?)\\s+([FPE\\.]+)\\s+\\[\\s*\\d+%?\\]'\n\n    # Assertion patterns\n    ASSERT_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s+assert\\s+(.+)'\n    EQUALITY_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s+assert\\s+(.+?)\\s+==\\s+(.+)'\n    CONTAINS_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s+assert\\s+(.+?)\\s+in\\s+(.+)'\n    TYPE_CHECK_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s+assert\\s+isinstance\\((.+?),\\s*(.+?)\\)'\n\n    # Diff patterns\n    DIFF_MINUS_PATTERN = r'^\\s*-\\s*(.+)'\n    DIFF_PLUS_PATTERN = r'^\\s*\\+\\s*(.+)'\n    DIFF_POSITION_PATTERN = r'^\\s*\\?\\s+(.+)'\n    INDEX_DIFF_PATTERN = r'^\\s+At\\s+index\\s+(\\d+)\\s+diff:\\s+(.+?)\\s+!=\\s+(.+)'\n    DICT_DIFF_HEADER = r'^\\s+Differing items:'\n    DICT_DIFF_LINE = r'^\\s+\\{(.+?)\\}\\s+!=\\s+\\{(.+?)\\}'\n    SET_DIFF_LEFT = r'^\\s+Extra items in the left set:'\n    SET_DIFF_RIGHT = r'^\\s+Extra items in the right set:'\n    RANGE_DIFF_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(?:E\\s+)?(Right|Left) contains (?:one more item|\\d+ more items?)\\s*:\\s*(.+)'\n    WHERE_CLAUSE_PATTERN = r'^\\s*\\+\\s+where\\s+(.+?)\\s+=\\s+(.+)'\n\n    # Section markers\n    FAILURES_SECTION = r'^===+ FAILURES ===+'\n    SUMMARY_SECTION = r'^===+ short test summary info ===+'\n    SESSION_START = r'^===+ test session starts ===+'\n    SESSION_END = r'^===+ \\d+ (?:failed|passed) in [\\d.]+s ===+'\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.failures: List[TestFailure] = []\n\n    def parse(self, output: str) -> List[TestFailure]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Parse pytest output and return structured failure data.\n\n        Handles multiple pytest output formats:\n        - --tb=line: One line per failure with file:line: AssertionError: message\n        - --tb=short: Multi-line failures with detailed diffs\n        - --tb=no: Only summary section\n\n        Args:\n            output: Raw pytest output string\n\n        Returns:\n            List of TestFailure objects\n        \"\"\"\n        self.failures = []\n\n        # First, check if this is --tb=line format (no underscore headers)\n        if self._parse_line_format(output):\n            return self.failures\n\n        # Otherwise, parse with --tb=short format (has underscore headers)\n        self._parse_short_format(output)\n\n        return self.failures\n\n    def _parse_line_format(self, output: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Parse --tb=line format.\n\n        Format: file:line: AssertionError: message\n        Each failure is on a single line.\n        \"\"\"\n        lines = output.split('\\n')\n        failures_found = False\n\n        for line in lines:\n            match = re.match(self.LINE_FAILURE_PATTERN, line)\n            if match:\n                failures_found = True\n                file_path, line_num, error_message = match.groups()\n\n                # Extract test name from the path if available\n                test_name = self._extract_test_name_from_path(file_path)\n\n                failure = TestFailure(\n                    test_file=file_path,\n                    line_number=int(line_num),\n                    test_name=test_name,\n                    error_message=error_message.strip(),\n                    error_type=\"AssertionError\"\n                )\n\n                # Try to extract expected/actual from error message\n                self._extract_error_message_details(failure)\n\n                self.failures.append(failure)\n\n        return failures_found\n\n    def _parse_short_format(self, output: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Parse --tb=short format with underscore headers.\n\n        Format:\n        _________________________ test_name ___________________________\n        file:line: in test_name\n            assert line\n        E   AssertionError: message\n        E   assert expr1 == expr2\n        E\n        E       - expected\n        E       + actual\n        \"\"\"\n        lines = output.split('\\n')\n        current_failure: Optional[TestFailure] = None\n        in_diff_section = False\n        in_differing_items = False\n\n        for i, line in enumerate(lines):\n            # Check for failure header (line with many underscores and test name)\n            if re.match(r'^_{20,}\\s+\\w+', line):\n                # Save previous failure if exists\n                if current_failure and current_failure.test_name:\n                    self.failures.append(current_failure)\n\n                # Extract test name from header\n                test_name = self._extract_test_name_from_header(line)\n                current_failure = TestFailure(test_name=test_name)\n                in_diff_section = False\n                in_differing_items = False\n                continue\n\n            if not current_failure:\n                continue\n\n            # Parse file:line: in test_name\n            match = re.match(self.SHORT_FAILURE_PATTERN, line)\n            if match:\n                current_failure.test_file = match.group(1)\n                current_failure.line_number = int(match.group(2))\n                current_failure.test_name = match.group(3) or current_failure.test_name\n                continue\n\n            # Parse assertion line\n            if re.match(r'^E?\\s+assert\\s+', line):\n                current_failure.assertion_line = line.strip()\n                # Classify assertion type\n                current_failure.assertion_type = self._classify_assertion(line)\n                # Try to extract expected/actual from assertion\n                self._extract_assertion_details(current_failure, line)\n                continue\n\n            # Parse AssertionError message\n            if line.startswith('E   AssertionError:'):\n                error_msg = line.replace('E   AssertionError:', '').strip()\n                if error_msg:\n                    current_failure.error_message = error_msg\n                continue\n\n            # Detect index diff\n            match = re.match(self.INDEX_DIFF_PATTERN, line)\n            if match:\n                current_failure.index_diff = int(match.group(1))\n                # Store the diff values\n                current_failure.expected = match.group(2).strip()\n                current_failure.actual = match.group(3).strip()\n                continue\n\n            # Detect differing items section\n            if re.match(self.DICT_DIFF_HEADER, line):\n                in_differing_items = True\n                in_diff_section = False\n                continue\n\n            # Parse dict diff items\n            if in_differing_items:\n                match = re.match(self.DICT_DIFF_LINE, line)\n                if match:\n                    # Parse key-value pairs from the diff\n                    try:\n                        left_dict = self._parse_dict_value(match.group(1))\n                        right_dict = self._parse_dict_value(match.group(2))\n                        for key, left_val in left_dict.items():\n                            right_val = right_dict.get(key)\n                            if right_val and left_val != right_val:\n                                current_failure.differing_items.append({\n                                    'key': key,\n                                    'expected': right_val,\n                                    'actual': left_val\n                                })\n                    except:\n                        pass\n                # End of differing items\n                if not line.strip().startswith('E') and '!=' not in line:\n                    in_differing_items = False\n                continue\n\n            # Detect full diff section\n            if 'Full diff:' in line or (in_diff_section and line.strip() and not line.startswith('E')):\n                in_diff_section = True\n                in_differing_items = False\n\n                # Parse diff lines\n                if line.strip().startswith('-'):\n                    content = re.sub(r'^\\s*-\\s*', '', line).strip()\n                    if content and not content.startswith('?'):\n                        if not current_failure.expected:\n                            current_failure.expected = content\n                        current_failure.diff_lines.append(f\"- {content}\")\n                elif line.strip().startswith('+'):\n                    content = re.sub(r'^\\s*\\+\\s*', '', line).strip()\n                    if content and not content.startswith('?'):\n                        if not current_failure.actual:\n                            current_failure.actual = content\n                        current_failure.diff_lines.append(f\"+ {content}\")\n                continue\n\n            # Parse where clause\n            match = re.match(self.WHERE_CLAUSE_PATTERN, line)\n            if match:\n                current_failure.where_clause = f\"{match.group(1).strip()} = {match.group(2).strip()}\"\n                continue\n\n        # Don't forget the last failure\n        if current_failure and current_failure.test_name:\n            self.failures.append(current_failure)\n\n    def parse_test_summary(self, output: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Parse the test summary section (FAILED lines at the end).\n\n        Returns:\n            Dict with summary information\n        \"\"\"\n        summary = {\n            'total_failed': 0,\n            'total_passed': 0,\n            'total_duration': 0.0,\n            'failures': []\n        }\n\n        lines = output.split('\\n')\n        in_summary = False\n\n        for line in lines:\n            # Find summary section\n            if re.search(self.SUMMARY_SECTION, line):\n                in_summary = True\n                continue\n\n            if in_summary:\n                # Parse FAILED lines\n                if line.startswith('FAILED'):\n                    parts = line.split()\n                    if len(parts) >= 2:\n                        test_path = parts[1]\n                        error_type = 'AssertionError'\n                        if len(parts) > 2:\n                            error_msg = ' '.join(parts[3:])\n                            if error_msg:\n                                error_type = error_msg.split(':')[0] if ':' in error_msg else error_type\n                        summary['failures'].append({\n                            'test_path': test_path,\n                            'error_type': error_type\n                        })\n                elif line.startswith('PASSED'):\n                    summary['total_passed'] += 1\n\n                # Parse final count line\n                match = re.search(r'(\\d+)\\s+(failed|passed)\\s+in\\s+([\\d.]+)s', line)\n                if match:\n                    count = int(match.group(1))\n                    status = match.group(2)\n                    duration = float(match.group(3))\n                    if status == 'failed':\n                        summary['total_failed'] = count\n                    elif status == 'passed':\n                        summary['total_passed'] = count\n                    summary['total_duration'] = duration\n\n        return summary\n\n    def _extract_test_name_from_header(self, line: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Extract test name from underscore header line.\"\"\"\n        # Remove underscores and whitespace\n        cleaned = re.sub(r'^_+|_+$', '', line).strip()\n        # Extract the middle part (test name)\n        match = re.search(r'(\\w+)', cleaned)\n        return match.group(1) if match else \"\"\n\n    def _extract_test_name_from_path(self, path: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Extract test name from file path if in ::test_name format.\"\"\"\n        if '::' in path:\n            return path.split('::')[-1]\n        return \"\"\n\n    def _classify_assertion(self, line: str) -> Optional[str]:\n        \"\"\"Classify assertion type based on pattern.\"\"\"\n        if re.match(self.EQUALITY_PATTERN, line):\n            return 'equality'\n        elif re.match(self.CONTAINS_PATTERN, line):\n            return 'contains'\n        elif re.match(self.TYPE_CHECK_PATTERN, line):\n            return 'type_check'\n        elif ' and ' in line or ' or ' in line:\n            return 'boolean'\n        elif 'isinstance' in line:\n            return 'type_check'\n        return 'unknown'\n\n    def _extract_assertion_details(self, failure: TestFailure, line: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Extract expected/actual values from assertion line.\"\"\"\n        # Try equality pattern: assert X == Y\n        match = re.match(self.EQUALITY_PATTERN, line)\n        if match:\n            failure.expected = match.group(2).strip()\n            failure.actual = match.group(1).strip()\n            return\n\n        # Try contains pattern: assert X in Y\n        match = re.match(self.CONTAINS_PATTERN, line)\n        if match:\n            failure.actual = match.group(1).strip()\n            failure.expected = f\"in {match.group(2).strip()}\"\n            return\n\n    def _extract_error_message_details(self, failure: TestFailure) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Extract expected/actual from error message if available.\"\"\"\n        msg = failure.error_message\n\n        # Look for patterns like \"Expected X, got Y\"\n        match = re.search(r\"Expected\\s+['\\\"]?([^'\\\"]+)['\\\"]?\\s*,\\s*got\\s+['\\\"]?([^'\\\"]+)['\\\"]?\", msg, re.IGNORECASE)\n        if match:\n            failure.expected = match.group(1).strip()\n            failure.actual = match.group(2).strip()\n            return\n\n        # Look for patterns like \"X != Y\"\n        match = re.search(r'(.+?)\\s*!=\\s*(.+)', msg)\n        if match:\n            failure.expected = match.group(2).strip()\n            failure.actual = match.group(1).strip()\n\n    def _parse_dict_value(self, value: str) -> Dict[str, str]:\n        \"\"\"Parse a simple dict value from diff line.\"\"\"\n        result = {}\n        # Simple parsing for 'key': value format\n        matches = re.findall(r\"'([^']+)'\\s*:\\s*([^,}]+)\", value)\n        for key, val in matches:\n            result[key.strip(\"'\\\" \")] = val.strip()\n        return result\n\n\ndef extract_json_compatible_data(failures: List[TestFailure]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:\n    \"\"\"Convert TestFailure objects to JSON-serializable dictionaries.\"\"\"\n    return [f.to_dict() for f in failures]\n\n\ndef main():\n    \"\"\"Main entry point for command-line usage.\"\"\"\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(\n        description='Parse pytest output and extract structured failure data',\n        formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter,\n        epilog=\"\"\"\nExamples:\n  # Parse from file\n  python parse_pytest_output.py output.txt\n\n  # Parse from stdin\n  pytest --tb=line test_file.py | python parse_pytest_output.py\n\n  # Output JSON\n  python parse_pytest_output.py --json output.txt\n\n  # Parse and save to file\n  python parse_pytest_output.py --json -o parsed.json output.txt\n        \"\"\"\n    )\n\n    parser.add_argument(\n        'input_file',\n        nargs='?',\n        help='Input file containing pytest output (reads from stdin if not provided)'\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--json',\n        action='store_true',\n        help='Output results as JSON instead of human-readable text'\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        '--output',\n        '-o',\n        help='Write output to file instead of stdout'\n    )\n\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    # Read input\n    try:\n        if args.input_file:\n            with open(args.input_file, 'r') as f:\n                output =        lines = []\n        lines.append(f\"=== Parsed {len(failures)} Failures ===\\n\")\n\n        for i, failure in enumerate(failures, 1):\n            lines.append(f\"Failure #{i}:\")\n output according to documented patterns,\nextracting file paths, line numbers, expected values, and actual values.\n\nAuthor: ARMOR Project (bf-29wbke)\nCreated: 2026-07-13\nBased on: pytest_patterns.md research (bf-5x5xz1)\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nimport re\nimport sys\nimport argparse\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom typing import List, Optional, Dict, Any\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass TestFailure:\n    \"\"\"Structured representation of a single test failure.\"\"\"\n    test_name: str = \"\"\n    test_file: str = \"\"\n    line_number: Optional[int] = None\n    error_type: str = \"AssertionError\"\n    error_message: str = \"\"\n    assertion_line: Optional[str] = None\n    assertion_type: Optional[str] = None\n    expected: Optional[str] = None\n    actual: Optional[str] = None\n    diff_lines: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n    index_diff: Optional[int] = None\n    differing_items: List[Dict[str, str]] = field(default_factory=list)\n    where_clause: Optional[str] = None\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Convert to JSON-serializable dictionary.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            'test_name': self.test_name,\n            'test_file': self.test_file,\n            'line_number': self.line_number,\n            'error_type': self.error_type,\n            'error_message': self.error_message,\n            'assertion_line': self.assertion_line,\n            'assertion_type': self.assertion_type,\n            'expected': self.expected,\n            'actual': self.actual,\n            'diff_lines': self.diff_lines,\n            'index_diff': self.index_diff,\n            'differing_items': self.differing_items,\n            'where_clause': self.where_clause\n        }\n\n\nclass PytestOutputParser:\n    \"\"\"Parser for pytest output using documented patterns from pytest_parser.md.\"\"\"\n\n    # Core patterns from pytest_parser.md\n    FILE_LOCATION_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):'\n    SHORT_FAILURE_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):\\s+in\\s+(\\w+)'\n    LINE_FAILURE_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?):(\\d+):\\s+AssertionError:\\s*(.+)'\n    VERBOSE_TEST_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?)::(\\w+)\\s+(FAILED|PASSED|ERROR|SKIPPED)\\s+\\[\\s*\\d+%?\\]'\n    CONCISE_TEST_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(.+?)\\s+([FPE\\.]+)\\s+\\[\\s*\\d+%?\\]'\n\n    # Assertion patterns\n    ASSERT_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s+assert\\s+(.+)'\n    EQUALITY_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s+assert\\s+(.+?)\\s+==\\s+(.+)'\n    CONTAINS_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s+assert\\s+(.+?)\\s+in\\s+(.+)'\n    TYPE_CHECK_PATTERN = r'^E?\\s+assert\\s+isinstance\\((.+?),\\s*(.+?)\\)'\n\n    # Diff patterns\n    DIFF_MINUS_PATTERN = r'^\\s*-\\s*(.+)'\n    DIFF_PLUS_PATTERN = r'^\\s*\\+\\s*(.+)'\n    DIFF_POSITION_PATTERN = r'^\\s*\\?\\s+(.+)'\n    INDEX_DIFF_PATTERN = r'^\\s+At\\s+index\\s+(\\d+)\\s+diff:\\s+(.+?)\\s+!=\\s+(.+)'\n    DICT_DIFF_HEADER = r'^\\s+Differing items:'\n    DICT_DIFF_LINE = r'^\\s+\\{(.+?)\\}\\s+!=\\s+\\{(.+?)\\}'\n    SET_DIFF_LEFT = r'^\\s+Extra items in the left set:'\n    SET_DIFF_RIGHT = r'^\\s+Extra items in the right set:'\n    RANGE_DIFF_PATTERN = r'^\\s*(?:E\\s+)?(Right|Left) contains (?:one more item|\\d+ more items?)\\s*:\\s*(.+)'\n    WHERE_CLAUSE_PATTERN = r'^\\s*\\+\\s+where\\s+(.+?)\\s+=\\s+(.+)'\n\n    # Section markers\n    FAILURES_SECTION = r'^===+ FAILURES ===+'\n    SUMMARY_SECTION = r'^===+ short test summary info ===+'\n    SESSION_START = r'^===+ test session starts ===+'\n    SESSION_END = r'^===+ \\d+ (?:failed|passed) in [\\d.]+s ===+'\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.failures: List[TestFailure] = []\n\n    def parse(self, output: str) -> List[TestFailure]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Parse pytest output and return structured failure data.\n\n        Handles multiple pytest output formats:\n        - --tb=line: One line per failure with file:line: AssertionError: message\n        - --tb=short: Multi-line failures with detailed diffs\n        - --tb=no: Only summary section\n\n        Args:\n            output: Raw pytest output string\n\n        Returns:\n            List of TestFailure objects\n        \"\"\"\n        self.failures = []\n\n        # First, check if this is --tb=line format (no underscore headers)\n        if self._parse_line_format(output):\n            return self.failures\n\n        # Otherwise, parse with --tb=short format (has underscore headers)\n        self._parse_short_format(output)\n\n        return self.failures\n\n    def _parse_line_format(self, output: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Parse --tb=line format.\n\n        Format: file:line: AssertionError: message\n        Each failure is on a single line.\n        \"\"\"\n        lines = output.split('\\n')\n        failures_found = False\n\n        for line in lines:\n            match = re.match(self.LINE_FAILURE_PATTERN, line)\n            if match:\n                failures_found = True\n                file_path, line_num, error_message = match.groups()\n\n                # Extract test name from the path if available\n                test_name = self._extract_test_name_from_path(file_path)\n\n                failure = TestFailure(\n                    test_file=file_path,\n                    line_number=int(line_num),\n                    test_name=test_name,\n                    error_message=error_message.strip(),\n                    error_type=\"AssertionError\"\n                )\n\n                # Try to extract expected/actual from error message\n                self._extract_error_message_details(failure)\n\n                self.failures.append(failure)\n\n        return failures_found\n\n    def _parse_short_format(self, output: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Parse --tb=short format with underscore headers.\n\n        Format:\n        _________________________ test_name ___________________________\n        file:line: in test_name\n            assert line\n        E   AssertionError: message\n        E   assert expr1 == expr2\n        E\n        E       - expected\n        E       + actual\n        \"\"\"\n        lines = output.split('\\n')\n        current_failure: Optional[TestFailure] = None\n        in_diff_section = False\n        in_differing_items = False\n\n        for i, line in enumerate(lines):\n            # Check for failure header (line with many underscores and test name)\n            if re.match(r'^_{20,}\\s+\\w+', line):\n                # Save previous failure if exists\n                if current_failure and current_failure.test_name:\n                    self.failures.append(current_failure)\n\n                # Extract test name from header\n                test_name = self._extract_test_name_from_header(line)\n                current_failure = TestFailure(test_name=test_name)\n                in_diff_section = False\n                in_differing_items = False\n                continue\n\n            if not current_failure:\n                continue\n\n            # Parse file:line: in test_name\n            match = re.match(self.SHORT_FAILURE_PATTERN, line)\n            if match:\n                current_failure.test_file = match.group(1)\n                current_failure.line_number = int(match.group(2))\n                current_failure.test_name = match.group(3) or current_failure.test_name\n                continue\n\n            # Parse assertion line\n            if re.match(r'^E?\\s+assert\\s+', line):\n                current_failure.assertion_line = line.strip()\n                # Classify assertion type\n                current_failure.assertion_type = self._classify_assertion(line)\n                # Try to extract expected/actual from assertion\n                self._extract_assertion_details(current_failure, line)\n                continue\n\n            # Parse AssertionError message\n            if line.startswith('E   AssertionError:'):\n                error_msg = line.replace('E   AssertionError:', '').strip()\n                if error_msg:\n                    current_failure.error_message = error_msg\n                continue\n\n            # Detect index diff\n            match = re.match(self.INDEX_DIFF_PATTERN, line)\n            if match:\n                current_failure.index_diff = int(match.group(1))\n                # Store the diff values\n                current_failure.expected = match.group(2).strip()\n                current_failure.actual = match.group(3).strip()\n                continue\n\n            # Detect differing items section\n            if re.match(self.DICT_DIFF_HEADER, line):\n                in_differing_items = True\n                in_diff_section = False\n                continue\n\n            # Parse dict diff items\n            if in_differing_items:\n                match = re.match(self.DICT_DIFF_LINE, line)\n                if match:\n                    # Parse key-value pairs from the diff\n                    try:\n                        left_dict = self._parse_dict_value(match.group(1))\n                        right_dict = self._parse_dict_value(match.group(2))\n                        for key, left_val in left_dict.items():\n                            right_val = right_dict.get(key)\n                            if right_val and left_val != right_val:\n                                current_failure.differing_items.append({\n                                    'key': key,\n                                    'expected': right_val,\n                                    'actual': left_val\n                                })\n                    except:\n                        pass\n                # End of differing items\n                if not line.strip().startswith('E') and '!=' not in line:\n                    in_differing_items = False\n                continue\n\n            # Detect full diff section\n            if 'Full diff:' in line or (in_diff_section and line.strip() and not line.startswith('E')):\n                in_diff_section = True\n                in_differing_items = False\n\n                # Parse diff lines\n                if line.strip().startswith('-'):\n                    content = re.sub(r'^\\s*-\\s*', '', line).strip()\n                    if content and not content.startswith('?'):\n                        if not current_failure.expected:\n                            current_failure.expected = content\n                        current_failure.diff_lines.append(f\"- {content}\")\n                elif line.strip().startswith('+'):\n                    content = re.sub(r'^\\s*\\+\\s*', '', line).strip()\n                    if content and not content.startswith('?'):\n                        if not current_failure.actual:\n                            current_failure.actual = content\n                        current_failure.diff_lines.append(f\"+ {content}\")\n                continue\n\n            # Parse where clause\n            match = re.match(self.WHERE_CLAUSE_PATTERN, line)\n            if match:\n                current_failure.where_clause = f\"{match.group(1).strip()} = {match.group(2).strip()}\"\n                continue\n\n        # Don't forget the last failure\n        if current_failure and current_failure.test_name:\n            self.failures.append(current_failure)\n\n    def parse_test_summary(self, output: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Parse the test summary section (FAILED lines at the end).\n\n        Returns:\n            Dict with summary information\n        \"\"\"\n        summary = {\n            'total_failed': 0,\n            'total_passed': 0,\n            'total_duration': 0.0,\n            'failures': []\n        }\n\n        lines = output.split('\\n')\n        in_summary = False\n\n        for line in lines:\n            # Find summary section\n            if re.search(self.SUMMARY_SECTION, line):\n                in_summary = True\n                continue\n\n            if in_summary:\n                # Parse FAILED lines\n                if line.startswith('FAILED'):\n                    parts = line.split()\n                    if len(parts) >= 2:\n                        test_path = parts[1]\n                        error_type = 'AssertionError'\n                        if len(parts) > 2:\n                            error_msg = ' '.join(parts[3:])\n                            if error_msg:\n                                error_type = error_msg.split(':')[0] if ':' in error_msg else error_type\n                        summary['failures'].append({\n                            'test_path': test_path,\n                            'error_type': error_type\n                        })\n                elif line.startswith('PASSED'):\n                    summary['total_passed'] += 1\n\n                # Parse final count line\n                match = re.search(r'(\\d+)\\s+(failed|passed)\\s+in\\s+([\\d.]+)s', line)\n                if match:\n                    count = int(match.group(1))\n                    status = match.group(2)\n                    duration = float(match.group(3))\n                    if status == 'failed':\n                        summary['total_failed'] = count\n                    elif status == 'passed':\n                        summary['total_passed'] = count\n                    summary['total_duration'] = duration\n\n        return summary\n\n    def _extract_test_name_from_header(self, line: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Extract test name from underscore header line.\"\"\"\n        # Remove underscores and whitespace\n        cleaned = re.sub(r'^_+|_+$', '', line).strip()\n        # Extract the middle part (test name)\n        match = re.search(r'(\\w+)', cleaned)\n        return match.group(1) if match else \"\"\n\n    def _extract_test_name_from_path(self, path: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Extract test name from file path if in ::test_name format.\"\"\"\n        if '::' in path:\n            return path.split('::')[-1]\n        return \"\"\n\n    def _classify_assertion(self, line: str) -> Optional[str]:\n        \"\"\"Classify assertion type based on pattern.\"\"\"\n        if re.match(self.EQUALITY_PATTERN, line):\n            return 'equality'\n        elif re.match(self.CONTAINS_PATTERN, line):\n            return 'contains'\n        elif re.match(self.TYPE_CHECK_PATTERN, line):\n            return 'type_check'\n        elif ' and ' in line or ' or ' in line:\n            return 'boolean'\n        elif 'isinstance' in line:\n            return 'type_check'\n        return 'unknown'\n\n    def _extract_assertion_details(self, failure: TestFailure, line: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Extract expected/actual values from assertion line.\"\"\"\n        # Try equality pattern: assert X == Y\n        match = re.match(self.EQUALITY_PATTERN, line)\n        if match:\n            failure.expected = match.group(2).strip()\n            failure.actual = match.group(1).strip()\n            return\n\n        # Try contains pattern: assert X in Y\n        match = re.match(self.CONTAINS_PATTERN, line)\n        if match:\n            failure.actual = match.group(1).strip()\n            failure.expected = f\"in {match.group(2).strip()}\"\n            return\n\n    def _extract_error_message_details(self, failure: TestFailure) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Extract expected/actual from error message if available.\"\"\"\n        msg = failure.error_message\n\n        # Look for patterns like \"Expected X, got Y\"\n        match = re.search(r\"Expected\\s+['\\\"]?([^'\\\"]+)['\\\"]?\\s*,\\s*got\\s+['\\\"]?([^'\\\"]+)['\\\"]?\", msg, re.IGNORECASE)\n        if match:\n            failure.expected = match.group(1).strip()\n            failure.actual = match.group(2).strip()\n            return\n\n        # Look for patterns like \"X != Y\"\n        match = re.search(r'(.+?)\\s*!=\\s*(.+)', msg)\n        if match:\n            failure.expected = match.group(2).strip()\n            failure.actual = match.group(1).strip()\n\n    def _parse_dict_value(self, value: str) -> Dict[str, str]:\n        \"\"\"Parse a simple dict value from diff line.\"\"\"\n        result = {}\n        # Simple parsing for 'key': value format\n        matches = re.findall(r\"'([^']+)'\\s*:\\s*([^,}]+)\", value)\n        for key, val in TestFailure objects to JSON-serializable dictionaries.\"\"\"\n    return [f.to_dict() for f in failures]\n\n\ndef main():\n    \"\"\"Main entry point for command-line usage.\"\"\"\n    parser =          lines.append(f\"  Index Diff: {failure.index_diff}\")\n            if failure.differing_items:\n                lines.append(f\"  Differing Items: {len(failure.differing_items)}\")\n                for item in failure.differing_items:\n                    lines.append(f\"    - {item['key']}: expected={item['expected']}, actual={item['actual']}\")\n            if failure.where_clause:\n script parses pytest failure output according to documented patterns,\nextracting file paths, line numbers, expected values, and actual values.\n\nAuthor: ARMOR Project (bf-29wbke)\nCreated: 2026-07-13\nBased on: pytest_patterns.md research (bf-5x5xz1)\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nimport re\nimport sys\nimport argparse\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom typing import List, Optional, Dict, Any\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass TestFailure:\n    \"\"\"Structured representation of a single test failure.\"\"\"\n    test_name: str = \"\"\n    test_file: str = \"\"\n    line_number: Optional[int] = None\n    error_type: str = \"AssertionError\"\n    error_message: str = \"\"\n    assertion_line: Optional[str] = None\n    assertion_type:
